sutta: the buddha is saying verily monks i say asaba destruction depends on the first jhana thoroughly i say asava destruction depends on the second jana on the third on the fourth jana on the sphere of infinite space sphere of infinite consciousness on the sphere of nothingness verily i say asaba destruction depends on the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception manga city said i say so and wherefore is this said consider the monk who aloo from sen's desires allude from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first ghana whatever occurs there a form feeling perception volition or consciousness he sees wholly as impermanent as suffering as a disease [Music] a tumor the calamity and affliction as something alien disintegrating empty not self he turns his mind away from such conditions and having done so brings the mind towards the deathless element with the thought this is the peace this is the summit justice the tranquilization of all conditioners the renouncing of all rebirth pieces the destroying of craving passionlessness ending the cool and steadfast therein he wins to asava destruction if not just by reason of that dumber delight that dhamma sweetness he snaps the 5 lower factors and is born spontaneously and being not subject to return from that world becomes completely cool there man suppose an archer or his people were to practice on a straw man or heap of clay presently he would become a long shot a rapid shot a piercer of great thicknesses even so amongst the monk who allude from sen's desires enters and abides in the first jhana and sees all form feeling perception volition consciousness as impermanent as suffering etc as not self turns his mind away from that and brings it towards the deathless element with the thought this is the peace this is the summit justice the tranquilization of all conditioners the renouncing of all rebirth bases the destroying of craving passionately ending the cool he wins to asaba destruction or snapping the 5 lower fatters is born spontaneously and becomes completely cool verily monks it is said i say asa destruction depends on the first jhana and it is for this reason that it is monks it is said i say asava destruction defends on the second the third the fourth jhana on the sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness sphere of nothingness and wherefore consider the monk who enters and abides in the second jana third jana fought jhana sphere of infinite space sea of infinite consciousness fear of nothingness whatever occurs there of form feeling perception volition and consciousness he sees wholly as impermanent as suffering etc he turns his mind away from such conditions and brings the mind towards the deathless element and he wins to assava destruction or snaps the 5 lower factors and it is for this reason that i said asava destruction depends on the jaanas thus monks as far as perception prevails there is knowledge penetration moreover monks these shears both the attainment of the sphere of neither perception or non-perception and the ending of perception and feeling are ones which i say on in emerging there from after they have attained and emerged there from that's the end of the suta
commentary: so this is another very important suta where the buddha is saying that the attainment of arahan hudna which means the destruction of the asavas the asavasa the uncontrolled mental outflows depends on the first jhana depends on the second jana the third the fourth jhana and the arupa janas infinite space infinite consciousness nothingness neither perception or non-perception and even the ending of perception and feeling so here it is very clear from the buddha's teachings here that attainment of arahan must depend on the jarnas but nowadays there are some teachings and we say that jhana is not necessary and this contradicts the suta here as well as some other suta we heard earlier where the buddha said concentration is the way no concentration the wrong way and here the buddha explains why buddha says if a monk enters the jhanas then he's able to see the 5 aggregates form feeling perception volition or consciousness as impermanent as suffering as a disease as a tumor a dart dark calamity and affliction as something alien disintegrating empty not self and so he turns his mind away from such conditions and having done so brings the mind towards the deathless element saying that this is the peace this is the summit namely the tranquilization of all conditioners the palis saba sankara samato why this word samata is tranquilization it is not inside tranquilization of all conditions the renouncing of all bases the destroying of craving compassionlessness ending the cool and having brought the mind to the state of complete tranquilization [Music] then he attains to asava destruction or if he does not attain then he destroys the 5 lower factors and becomes an anagami a third fruition person who is born spontaneously and not does not return from that world to this world the nexuta
